creator: brian@brianwhalen.net
text:
I recently tried zfs on a stable/14 and ccache -s appears to be working. I installed a 14.1 system virtually with 2 disks in a zfs mirror. I then ran freebsd-update fetch and install and then upgraded to the stable version.

root@f14bigdisk:~ # df -h
Filesystem            Size    Used   Avail Capacity  Mounted on
zroot/ROOT/default    940G    8.0G    932G     1%    /
devfs                 1.0K      0B    1.0K     0%    /dev
zroot                 932G     96K    932G     0%    /zroot
zroot/home            932G     96K    932G     0%    /home
zroot/var/crash       932G     96K    932G     0%    /var/crash
zroot/var/audit       932G     96K    932G     0%    /var/audit
zroot/var/tmp         932G     96K    932G     0%    /var/tmp
zroot/var/log         932G    200K    932G     0%    /var/log
zroot/var/mail        932G    108K    932G     0%    /var/mail
zroot/usr/ports       935G    2.2G    932G     0%    /usr/ports
zroot/home/brian      932G    128K    932G     0%    /home/brian
zroot/tmp             932G    268K    932G     0%    /tmp
zroot/usr/src         935G    2.5G    932G     0%    /usr/src
root@f14bigdisk:~ # ccache -s
cache directory                     /root/.ccache
primary config                      /root/.ccache/ccache.conf
secondary config      (readonly)    /usr/local/etc/ccache.conf
stats updated                       Sat Jun 29 18:39:46 2024
stats zeroed                        Sat Jun 29 18:30:09 2024
cache hit (direct)                  7518
cache hit (preprocessed)            2246
cache miss                           644
cache hit rate                     93.81 %
cleanups performed                     0
files in cache                    173666
cache size                           3.2 GB
max cache size                      10.0 GB
